171	/-- **Leg-B atomic claim: the lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars` along the open-immersion
172	structure ring iso `(f.appIso W').inv` is an isomorphism.**  Its underlying map is the (bijective)
173	ring map `(f.appIso W').inv.hom`, so `ε` is an iso by `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`
174	(`PresheafInternalHom.lean`) fed the bijectivity from `ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso`.  This
175	is the single load-bearing fact powering `dualUnitRingSwap` (the codomain unit ring swap of leg-B),
176	phrased at the `CommRingCat` carrier so `CommRing` is native (per `analogies/ma-legb262.md`). -/
177	lemma isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
178	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
179	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
180	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom)) :=
181	  restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom
182	    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv)

185	/-- **Leg-B: the codomain unit ring-iso swap** `restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv (𝟙_X(fW')) ⟶
186	𝟙_Y(W')`.  It is the inverse of the lax-monoidal unit `ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom)`,
187	an isomorphism by `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso`.  The endpoints are written at the canonical
188	`CommRingCat` section carriers `↑(X.presheaf.obj _)` / `↑(Y.presheaf.obj _)` (the `forget₂`-composite
189	carrier breaks `MonoidalCategoryStruct` synthesis, `analogies/ma-legb262.md`); they reconcile by
190	`rfl`/defeq with the `restr`/`𝟙_`-section spellings of `sliceDualTransport`'s `codomainMap` hole. -/
191	noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
192	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
193	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom).obj
194	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W'))))) ⟶
195	      𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj (op W'))) :=
196	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso f W'
197	  CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
198	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom))

201	/-- **Leg (A)∘(B): the sectionwise slice transport of the dual along an open immersion.**
202	
203	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X`, `M : X.Modules`, and an open `V` of `Y` (as `(Opens Y)ᵒᵖ`),
204	this is the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear isomorphism between the two sectionwise values of the Step-4 residual
205	of `dual_restrict_iso`:
206	```
207	  ((pushforward β).obj (dual M.val)).obj V  ≅  (dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V
208	```
209	where `β` is the open-immersion structure ring morphism `Y.ringCatSheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙
210	X.ringCatSheaf` (`β.app U = (forget₂ _ _).map (f.appIso U).inv`).
211	
212	The construction mirrors `homLocalSection` (the thin-poset `eqToHom`-conjugation slice transport)
213	composed with `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` (the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear codomain-unit ring swap of leg
214	(B)): a dual section `φ : restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X` over `Over (fV)` is reindexed across
215	`f.opensFunctor` to a dual section over `Over V`, conjugating each component by the structure ring
216	iso `f.appIso`; naturality on the thin poset `Opens Y` is `Subsingleton.elim`. -/
217	noncomputable def sliceDualTransport {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
218	    (M : X.Modules) (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y)ᵒᵖ) :
219	    letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
220	      { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
221	    letI β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
222	      Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
223	    (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj (PresheafOfModules.dual M.val)).obj V) ≅
224	      ((PresheafOfModules.dual ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V) := by
225	  -- CONSTRUCTION PLAN (homLocalSection-style leg (A) ∘ restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv leg (B)):
226	  --
227	  -- Write `fV := f.opensFunctor.obj V.unop`.  By `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_obj`,
228	  --   LHS carrier `L = (dual M.val).obj (op fV) = (restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X)`,
229	  --     a `𝒪_X(fV)`-module restricted along `β.app V : 𝒪_Y(V) ⟶ 𝒪_X(fV)` to a `𝒪_Y(V)`-module;
230	  --   RHS carrier `Rr = (restr V.unop ((pushforward β).obj M.val) ⟶ restr V.unop 𝟙_Y)`,
231	  --     a `𝒪_Y(V)`-module via `internalHomObjModule`.
232	  --
233	  -- Build a `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear equivalence `e : L ≃ₗ[𝒪_Y(V)] Rr` and return `e.toModuleIso`.

236	  -- whose component at `W : (Over V.unop)ᵒᵖ` (so `W' := W.unop.left ≤ V.unop`, with image

241	  -- packaged as a `ModuleCat` hom over `𝒪_Y(W')`.  Naturality of this family in `W` is automatic

358	/-- **Restriction along an open immersion commutes with the sheaf-level dual (C-bridge).**
359	
360	Blueprint `lem:dual_restrict_iso` (§`sec:tensorobj_dual_bridge`).  For an open immersion
361	`f : Y ⟶ X` and `M : X.Modules`, there is a canonical isomorphism of `𝒪_Y`-modules
362	```
363	  (dual M).restrict f  ≅  dual (M.restrict f)
364	```
365	natural in `M`, between the restriction of the sheaf-level dual and the dual of the
366	restriction.
367	
368	/- Planner strategy:
369	   Blueprint label: lem:dual_restrict_iso (~L5374).
370	
371	   Proof-sketch (blueprint §5.4):
372	   The proof runs at the PRESHEAF-OF-MODULES level (Step 3 of the tensorObj_restrict_iso
373	   H1∘H2 recipe already strips the outer sheafification).  Three ingredients:
374	
375	   (a) Per-V slice equivalence: for each V ≤ U (= image of f), the opens functor
376	       `f.opensFunctor` is fully faithful with image = {W ≤ U}, so
377	       `Over_Y V ≃ Over_X (f.opensFunctor V)`.  This is the per-open shadow of
378	       `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence` (CLOSED in Vestigial.lean via
379	       `overSliceSheafEquiv`).
380	
381	   (b) Agreement of codomain: the structure sheaf of Y agrees with that of X under (a).
382	
383	   (c) Ring-iso transport of module structure:
384	       `lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv` (CLOSED in PresheafInternalHom.lean as
385	       `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`):
386	         `RingEquiv e → Dual(restrictScalars e.toRingHom A) ≃ restrictScalars e.toRingHom (Dual A)`
387	       applies sectionwise at each V to transport the `𝒪_X(fV)`-module structure on
388	       `(dual M)|_f(V)` to the `𝒪_Y(V)`-module structure via the ring iso
389	       `β_V = (f.appIso V).inv : 𝒪_X(fV) ≅ 𝒪_Y(V)`.
390	
391	   High-level recipe (mirrors tensorObj_restrict_iso Steps 1–4 with `dual` in place of `⊗`):
392	   Step 1: `(Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (dual M)` — reduce `restrict`
393	           to abstract pullback.
394	   Step 2: `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom` — move pullback
395	           inside sheafification.
396	   Step 3: strip the outer sheafification via `(sheafification …).mapIso`.

571	  refine ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
572	  exact dual_restrict_iso U.ι L ≪≫ (dualIsoOfIso eL).symm ≪≫ dual_unit_iso

592	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
593	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr hle

595	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left)) ≫

598	    intro A B φ
599	    have hBA : (unop B).left ≤ (unop A).left := ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).le
600	    let κ : (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left ⟶ (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left :=
601	      (Opens.map (U i).ι.base).map (homOfLE hBA)
602	    have himgA : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left) = (unop A).left := by
603	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
604	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop A).hom.le
605	    have himgB : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left) = (unop B).left := by
606	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]

26	## Decisions identified
27	
28	### Decision: is `internalHomObjModule` a parallel re-implementation of a Mathlib module-on-Hom?
29	
30	- **Mathlib idiom**: the module structure on a `Hom`-type is built by supplying ONLY the
31	  scalar action on top of the *ambient* additive group; `add`/`0`/`neg` are NOT re-declared, they
32	  come from the category's `Preadditive` instance (`CategoryTheory.Preadditive`,
33	  `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Preadditive/Basic.lean`).  This is exactly how Mathlib's own
34	  `Module.End`, `ModuleCat.restrictScalars`-images, and the fixed-ring internal hom work: the
35	  `+` on `M ⟶ N` is one canonical `AddCommGroup`, and a `Module R (M ⟶ N)` only adds `smul`.
36	- **Project's path**: `homModule` (PresheafInternalHom.lean:628) is a `Module … (M ⟶ N) where`
37	  that supplies **only** `smul := fun f φ => φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f` and the eight `smul`-axiom
38	  fields.  It declares NO `add`/`zero`/`neg`.  The `AddCommGroup (M ⟶ N)` is the ambient
39	  `PresheafOfModules.Hom` Preadditive group.  `internalHomObjModule` is just `homModule`
40	  specialised to the slice terminal `Over.mkIdTerminal`.
41	- **Gap**: identical.  The `+` carried by `internalHomObjModule` IS the `PresheafOfModules.Hom`
42	  add — verified: `(x + y).app W = x.app W + y.app W` closes by `rfl` against the real objects
43	  (see Verified).  There is **no second add**; nothing parallel exists to unify.
44	- **Cost of divergence**: none — there is no divergence.
45	- **Verdict**: PROCEED.  `internalHomObjModule`/`homModule` is the Mathlib-aligned shape
46	  (scalar-only `Module` over a shared Preadditive group).  The `smul` (post-composition with
47	  `globalSMul`) is the mathematically-canonical action and mirrors `Module.End`/`LinearMap`
48	  composition; this is the right new structure, not a copy.
49	
50	### Decision: what bridge unblocks map_add' (and how far does it generalise)?
51	
52	- **Mathlib idiom**: when an `_app` / `_apply` rewrite (here `PresheafOfModules.add_app`) refuses
53	  to fire because the operand's `+` is on a `ModuleCat` *carrier* wrapped through
54	  `pushforward`/`restrictScalars`/`ofPresheaf` (rather than a literal `M ⟶ N` Hom add), the
55	  standard move is to **reshape the goal with `change`/`show` to the bare composite form first**,
56	  then let the additive-functor lemmas fire.  This carrier-vs-`Hom` defeq friction is endemic to
57	  all `PresheafOfModules`/`ModuleCat` work in Mathlib; `change` (not a new lemma) is the idiom.
58	- **Project's path (recommended)**: the directive's hypothesised "defeq bridge" is correct and is
59	  literally `rfl`.  No standalone helper lemma is required — an inline `show … from rfl` suffices.
60	  The load-bearing step is the `change` that precedes it.
61	- **Gap**: identical to the Mathlib idiom.
62	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (thin, rfl-level bridge; no API change).

72	  intro W
73	  -- LOAD-BEARING: `change … = _` reshapes BOTH sides so `Functor.map_add` can match the
74	  -- `(restrictScalars _).map (·)` application.  Without it (e.g. `simp only [add_app]`),
75	  -- `Functor.map_add` reports "pattern not found" even though the term displays identically.
76	  change (ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).map ((x + y).app _) ≫ _ = _
77	  rw [show (x + y).app (op (Over.mk ((Hom.opensFunctor f).map (unop W).hom)))
78	        = x.app (op (Over.mk ((Hom.opensFunctor f).map (unop W).hom)))
79	          + y.app (op (Over.mk ((Hom.opensFunctor f).map (unop W).hom))) from rfl,
80	      Functor.map_add, Preadditive.add_comp]
81	```
82	
83	Why each piece: the inline `show … from rfl` is the `internalHomObjModule`-add ↦ `Hom`-add bridge
84	(rfl); `ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ` is `Additive` (verified) so `Functor.map_add` applies; the
85	codomain swap `dualUnitRingSwap` is post-composed, so `Preadditive.add_comp` distributes it.
86	
87	**Generalisation**: the `change`-reshape front-half generalises to `map_smul'`, but the bridge
88	itself does NOT — `m • x` on the domain is the `homModule` post-composition action (`φ ≫ globalSMul`)
89	with the scalar at the `V`-level, NOT a pointwise `(β.app W m) • x.app W` (that naive `rfl` bridge
90	FAILS — verified).  `map_smul'` therefore needs the genuine smul argument: unfold the `homModule`
91	`smul` to `≫ globalSMul`, split with `comp_app`, evaluate via `globalSMul_hom_apply`, and intertwine
92	through `dualUnitRingSwap` (the presheaf shadow of `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`'s `map_smul'`).
93	It is more than a defeq bridge, but the same `change (ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).map ((m • x).app _) ≫ _ = _`
94	reshaping is the correct first step (verified to apply).
95	
96	The other sub-holes (`invFun`, `left_inv`, `right_inv`) are `Iso.inv_hom_id`/`hom_inv_id`
97	round-trips + down-set bijection; the add bridge does not bear on them (they are not additivity
98	obligations).  So the critic's "this recurs on every sub-hole" is only true for the `change`-reshape
99	pattern, which does recur and is cheap; the additive `rfl` bridge specifically recurs only where
100	additivity is at stake.
101	
102	Net: PROCEED.  No refactor.  The design is Mathlib-aligned; ship the verified `map_add'` recipe and
103	build `map_smul'` from the `homModule.smul`/`globalSMul` definition with the same `change` opener.

1	# Analogy: leg-B unit ring-iso swap (`inv (ε (restrictScalars β_W))`) — CommRing recovery + `𝟙_` section defeq
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	ma-legb262
8	
9	## Iteration
10	262
11	
12	## Question
13	For `sliceDualTransport`'s leg-B (the codomain unit ring-iso swap, intended term
14	`inv (ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom))`): (a) what is the Mathlib idiom for recovering
15	`CommRing` on a section ring viewed through `forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`; and (b) what is the
16	idiom for bridging `𝟙_ (ModuleCat S)` to the project's defeq presheaf-section spellings of the
17	same unit?
18	
19	## Project artifact(s)
20	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean:184-317` — `sliceDualTransport`,
21	  the `codomainMap` typed hole at L305.
22	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/PresheafInternalHom.lean:174-216` —
23	  `restrictScalars_isIso_ε` / `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective` (`[CommRing R][CommRing S]`).
24	- `PresheafInternalHom.lean:658-674` — `restr` / `internalHomObjModule` (the section spellings).
25	
26	## Decisions identified
27	
28	### Decision: how to obtain `CommRing` / apply the ε-iso lemma at the section ring (friction a)
29	
30	- **Mathlib idiom**: there is **no** instance that finds `CommRing` on a `forget₂ CommRingCat
31	  RingCat`-image carrier, and Mathlib does not want one — the idiom is to keep the object a
32	  `CommRingCat` (so its `CommRing` instance is the canonical one) and only forget to `RingCat`
33	  at the very last step. The relevant Mathlib facts: `ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η`
34	  (`Mathlib/.../ModuleCat/ChangeOfRings.lean`, the underlying map of `ε` is the ring map) and
35	  `CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso` (an iso's underlying map is bijective).
36	- **Verified by `lean_run_code`**:
37	  - `infer_instance : CommRing ↑(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj W)` **FAILS**; so does
38	    `CommRing ↑((forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj A)` for `A : CommRingCat`.
39	  - `change CommRing ↑(X.presheaf.obj W); infer_instance` **succeeds** (canonicalize to the
40	    `CommRingCat` carrier).
41	  - `((forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map g).hom = g.hom` is **`rfl`**, and
42	    `ModuleCat.restrictScalars ((forget₂ …).map g).hom = ModuleCat.restrictScalars g.hom` is
43	    **`rfl`**.  Since `β = whiskerRight α (forget₂ …)` with
44	    `α.app U = (f.appIso U.unop).inv` a **`CommRingCat`** morphism, `β.app U`'s restrictScalars
45	    is `rfl`-equal to `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom`.
46	  - `IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.inv.hom))` for a `CommRingCat`
47	    iso `e : A ≅ B` is provable verbatim by the `restrictScalars_isIso_ε` recipe
48	    (`bijective_of_isIso` → `RingEquiv.ofBijective` → `restrictScalars_η` → `RingEquiv.bijective`),
49	    with `CommRing ↑A`, `CommRing ↑B` found **natively** (A, B are `CommRingCat`).
50	- **Project's current path**: feed `β.app (op W')` (a `forget₂`'d `RingCat` hom) to
51	  `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`, which then fails to synthesize `CommRing` on the
52	  `RingCat`-carrier spelling.
53	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost (the friction is self-inflicted by forgetting to `RingCat` too
54	  early; no parallel API is needed).
55	- **Cost of divergence**: a hand-rolled "recover `CommRing` on a `forget₂` image" lemma would be
56	  a parallel API for something Mathlib deliberately routes around. The project already has the
57	  right lemma (`restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`); it just must be applied to the
58	  `CommRingCat`-level hom.
59	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB (use the `CommRingCat` iso; do **not** build a CommRing-recovery
60	  helper).
61	
62	### Decision: bridging `𝟙_ (ModuleCat S)` to the `restr`/`𝟙_X`-section unit spellings (friction b)
63	
64	- **Mathlib idiom**: there is **no** dedicated coherence lemma, and none is needed — the spellings
65	  are **definitionally equal**. The idiom is `show`/`change` to the canonical
66	  `𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(R.obj U))` form (carrier written as the **`CommRingCat`** section `↑(R.obj U)`),
67	  then `exact`.
68	- **Verified by `lean_run_code`**:
69	  - `(𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj U = 𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(R.obj U))`
70	    is **`rfl`**. (`restr U N`'s section at a slice object `W` is `N.obj (op W.left)` by
71	    `pushforward₀`'s `.obj` reduction, so a `restr`/`𝟙_X`-section of the unit is just `𝟙_X`
72	    evaluated, hence the same `rfl`.)
73	  - `inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.inv.hom))` typechecks with endpoints
74	    `(restrictScalars e.inv.hom).obj (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑A)) ⟶ 𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑B)` — exactly the
75	    canonical unit forms, so it unifies with the goal after canonicalization.
76	- **GOTCHA (verified)**: `MonoidalCategoryStruct (ModuleCat ↑((R ⋙ forget₂ …).obj U))` **FAILS**
77	  to synthesize even though plain `CommRing ↑((R ⋙ forget₂ …).obj U)` succeeds. So the unit must
78	  be written with the `↑(R.obj U)` (CommRingCat) carrier — at the `forget₂`-composite carrier the
79	  `𝟙_` notation itself cannot elaborate. `change`/`show` to `↑(R.obj U)` first.
80	- **Gap**: identical-up-to-defeq (no Mathlib lemma exists or is needed).
81	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (defeq via `show`/`change`; no transport lemma).
82	
83	### Decision: should the ε-iso-of-ring-equiv lemma come from Mathlib? (completeness)
84	
85	- **Mathlib idiom**: Mathlib ships `ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η` and
86	  `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsEquivalenceOfRingEquiv` (a `ModuleCat S ≌ ModuleCat R` for a ring
87	  equiv — a *categorical* equivalence, **not** the monoidal-unit `ε` iso). There is **no**
88	  `IsIso (ε (restrictScalars e))` / strong-monoidal-of-ring-equiv lemma in Mathlib.
89	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — already correctly filled by the project's
90	  `restrictScalars_isIso_ε` / `_of_bijective`. No new build required.
91	
92	## Recommendation
93	Phrase leg-B at the **`CommRingCat`** level, never recovering `CommRing` on the `RingCat`
94	spelling. Concretely in `sliceDualTransport`: reconstruct the `CommRingCat` natural transformation
95	`α` (or use `f.appIso W'` directly); set `g := (f.appIso W').inv.hom` (equivalently `(α.app
96	(op W')).hom`); obtain `hbij := ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (f.appIso W').inv`; supply
97	`haveI := restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective g hbij` (CommRing on the `CommRingCat` carriers is
98	native); take `codomainMap := CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars
99	g))`. Because `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (β.app _).hom = ModuleCat.restrictScalars g` is `rfl`, the
100	domain matches the leg-A composite. Finally `show`/`change` the goal's unit endpoints into
101	`(restrictScalars g).obj (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op fW')))) ⟶ 𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj
102	(op W')))` (canonical `↑(R.obj U)` carriers — the `forget₂`-composite carrier breaks the
103	`MonoidalCategoryStruct` synthesis) and `exact codomainMap`. No CommRing-recovery helper and no
104	unit-transport lemma should be written — both would be parallel APIs for what is already `rfl`.
105

# DualInverse.lean — fine-grained pass (iter-263)

Target: `sliceDualTransport` (L217) — close `map_add'` + `map_smul'` of the `≃ₗ`-packaging,
per the VERIFIED recipe in `analogies/ma-ihom263.md`. File compiles (errors: 0; sorries only).

## Sentence-by-sentence

### `map_add'`  → **RESOLVED** (axiom-clean, closes outright)
- Type: `∀ x y, toFun (x+y) = toFun x + toFun y` (the leg-A∘leg-B section family).
- Proof: the verified ma-ihom263 recipe — `intro x y; apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext; intro W;`
  `change (ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).map ((x+y).app _) ≫ _ = _;`
  `rw [show (x+y).app _ = x.app _ + y.app _ from rfl, Functor.map_add, Preadditive.add_comp]`
  **+ a trailing `rfl`** (the recipe's `rw` reshapes the LHS to the Preadditive sum; the RHS
  `(h₁+h₂).app W` is then defeq — `rfl` closes it; `PresheafOfModules.add_app` does NOT fire because
  the `+` is the `internalHomObjModule` instance, defeq-not-syntactic to the generic Hom add, but
  the whole equation IS defeq so `rfl` lands).
- The `internalHomObjModule`-add ↦ Hom-add bridge is genuinely `rfl` (ma-ihom263 confirmed; single
  shared Preadditive add).

### `map_smul'`  → **PARTIAL** (reduced from "blocked, unknown bridge" to a precise crux)
- Type: `∀ m x, toFun (m • x) = m • toFun x` (RHS scalar via `RingHom.id`).
- **Progress (measurable code advance)**: both `internalHomObjModule` smuls are now EXPOSED and the
  goal is reduced to the sectionwise crux. Concretely, after
  `intro m x; apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext; intro W;`
  `change (ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).map ((m • x).app _) ≫ _ = _ ≫ (globalSMul … ((RingHom.id _) m)).app W;`
  `erw [PresheafOfModules.comp_app]; apply ModuleCat.hom_ext; refine LinearMap.ext fun z => ?_;`
  `simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply, ModuleCat.restrictScalars.map_apply]`
  the goal is (with `u := (x.app W'').hom z`, `d := dualUnitRingSwap f W'`):
  ```
  d.hom (s • u) = c • (toFun-section).hom z      -- RHS ≡defeq  c • d.hom u
  ```
  where `s = termRingMap (Over fV') W'' ((β.app V) m)` (X-side) and `c = termRingMap (Over V) W m`.
- **Sole remaining content (NOT a structural wall — tactic friction):**
  1. β-naturality ring identity `s = (β.app W').hom c`
     (`InternalHom.termRingMap_naturality` + `β.naturality` on the thin poset `Opens Y`, matching the
     slice `termRingMap`s to base restriction along `f.opensFunctor`).
  2. `dualUnitRingSwap.hom` is `𝒪_Y(W')`-linear:
     `d.hom ((β.app W') c • u) = d.hom (c •_restr u) = c • d.hom u`
     via `ModuleCat.restrictScalars.smul_def'` (verified to fire, `←` direction) + `map_smul`.
- **BLOCKER (the exact failing step):** the RHS `(toFun-section).hom z` is a `{app := …}.app W`
  PROJECTION that is **defeq-but-not-syntactic** to `d.hom u`; `rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp]`, a
  hand-written `show … from rfl`, and `conv_rhs => rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp]` all report
  "pattern not found". Additionally `rw [hring]` fails because the goal's scalar carries the
  `{ toFun := (β.app V).hom.toFun, … } m` (MonoidWithZeroHom coercion) form rather than
  `(RingCat.Hom.hom (β.app V)) m`. Next pass: drive (1)+(2) via a `conv`/`change` that survives the
  projection, or finish with `exact hcrux` (defeq-tolerant) after stating `hcrux` in the matching
  syntactic form. All ingredient lemmas are identified and individually verified to fire.

### Other `sliceDualTransport` fields (untouched this pass — out of the map_add'/map_smul' bar)
- `naturality` (L335), `invFun` (L388), `left_inv` (L391), `right_inv` (L393) — typed sorries retained.
- `dual_restrict_iso` isoMk naturality (L524) — typed sorry retained.

## Summary
- **1/2 bar sentences closed**: `map_add'` RESOLVED axiom-clean; `map_smul'` reduced to the crux.
- **`sliceDualTransport` sub-sorries: 6 → 5** (map_add' eliminated).
- **decl-level `declaration uses sorry`: unchanged** (`sliceDualTransport` still open via the other 5
  fields; `dual_restrict_iso` still open).
- File compiles, 0 errors.

## Why I stopped
- **Real progress**: `map_add'` closed (1 sub-sorry eliminated, 6→5). This is half the directive bar
  and dissolves the "internalHomObjModule-add bridge" concern (it was `rfl`, as ma-ihom263 predicted).
- **Partial progress**: `map_smul'` advanced from a bare typed sorry with an unknown bridge to a fully
  exposed, single-line crux `d.hom (s • u) = c • d.hom u`, with BOTH the X-side and Y-side `globalSMul`
  actions unfolded (via `comp_app` + `globalSMul_hom_apply` + `ModuleCat.restrictScalars.map_apply`)
  and the complete finishing route identified (β-naturality `termRingMap_naturality`/`β.naturality`
  ring identity + `restrictScalars.smul_def'` + `map_smul`). The genuine blocker is the
  defeq-not-syntactic `{app}.app W` RHS projection that defeats `rw`/`show`/`conv_rhs` matching — a
  tactic-mechanics issue, not a missing mathematical ingredient. This is exactly the kind of residual
  that a focused `prove`/fine-grained pass on `map_smul'` alone should clear with the projection-
  surviving `change`/`exact hcrux` route noted above.
- **Housekeeping deferred**: the directive's stale-comment/docstring cleanups (iter-261 codomainMap
  "blocked" note ~L289, moving `/- Planner strategy -/` out of `/-- … -/` docstrings) were
  deprioritized in favor of the proof work; they are non-blocking comment-only edits (PROGRESS.md
  marks them "Non-blocking") and editing the large docstrings carries breakage risk with zero
  functional value. Recommend a dedicated cosmetic pass.

## Recommended next
- `prove` (or fine-grained) targeting ONLY `map_smul'` of `sliceDualTransport`, directive: finish the
  exposed crux `d.hom (s • u) = c • d.hom u` — (a) state `hring : s = (β.app W').hom c` via
  `termRingMap_naturality` + `β.naturality` (thin-poset slice→base `Subsingleton.elim` matching);
  (b) close via `rw [hring]` (normalize the `{toFun…} m` scalar first with `show`/`RingHom`-coe simp),
  `← ModuleCat.restrictScalars.smul_def'`, `map_smul`, then `rfl`/`exact` (defeq-tolerant for the RHS
  projection). All four lemmas verified to fire in isolation.

## Blueprint markers
- `lem:slice_dual_transport` — still has open fields; do NOT mark `\leanok` for the proof
  (statement-level `\leanok` is fine — the decl is formalized with sorries). `map_add'` closed.
